title: Imagine you are now 60 years old
display_title: Imagine you are now 60 years old
wikitext:
[]: Imagine you are now 60 years old~~             Quote Post by JinKazama2006 » Fri Dec 15, 2006 7:02 am Imagine you are now 60 years old and are in a house watching or discussing Evangelion with friends who watched and liked EVA and are at the same age as you are, while the younger ones look strangely at your group of old people, how weird it will be, so what do you reckon? When we all get old and stubborn in our old ways, imgaine when those days come, by then will we still be so "in love" with EVA? I think for me I am not going to change a bit. What about you guys and girls? When I'm 60 years old, the happiest day of my life at that age will definitely be a day when I meet someone of my age who also and still has been an EVA fan through all these years.

[]:             Quote Post by Dascoo » Fri Dec 15, 2006 7:27 am Yeah that would be pretty crazy, meeting someone like that...sadly, in real life I haven't met one Eva fan....yet.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Fri Dec 15, 2006 12:40 pm So, that gives me ten years to meet another Eva fan of my own age. Tricky.

[]:             Quote Post by The Bastard King » Fri Dec 15, 2006 12:55 pm If that's gonna be the case, then our generation is truly lost. Hell, if we were to see senior citizens doing as much with anime, even though we like it that would still be F'd up the A.

[]:             Quote Post by Soljer13 » Fri Dec 15, 2006 1:08 pm I can see it now! Senior Eva Fan: I remember when the fallout of Second Impact brought catastrophe to the world and Humanity. In the imidi... Oh God No!

[]:             Quote Post by Evangelionfreak » Fri Dec 15, 2006 3:05 pm well as cool as it would be to like Eva when Im old and grey I dont think its gonna happen. Even though i say things like "im an eva fan for life" who knows if I'll even still like anime when im older.

[]:             Quote Post by Hunter21 » Fri Dec 15, 2006 4:58 pm I have a feeling by the time I am 60 I will be more concerned with grandchildren, retirement, and medical issues to think back to some anime. It may be Eva but still it isn't nearly as important as reality based things in my life. So while it may not get completely forgotten I doubt I will think about all that much when I am 60.

[]:             Quote Post by tinmeigut » Sat Dec 16, 2006 9:13 pm Yeah that's right. Will I still be thinking about anime when I'm 60? Either I'll be counting how much money I have for the remaining part of my life or looking at my grandchildren play. Or in heaven.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sat Dec 16, 2006 9:18 pm I can't imagine being a huge Eva geek for another 37 years. I'll probably get sick of it at some point and only watch it once every year or two.

[]:             Quote Post by Kaysow » Sun Dec 17, 2006 10:00 am A few years might rekindle something. I saw Akira last week, and it was awesome again.

[]:             Quote Post by Kyle » Sun Dec 17, 2006 8:45 pm Man,if you guys are still gonna be watching eva when your that old i honestly hope youll be happy doing it.I plane to grow out of anime somtime soon.Id rather spend my golden years travaling then sit at home and watch anime. Besides the oil crises will happen sometime in the next 10 years or so and anime will be the last thing on your mind.

[]:             Quote Post by BobBQ » Sun Dec 17, 2006 8:54 pm Besides the oil crises will happen sometime in the next 10 years or so and anime will be the last thing on your mind.

[]:             Quote Post by Legend of the Past » Mon Dec 18, 2006 3:14 am soljerameelguy wrote: I can see it now! Senior Eva Fan: I remember when the fallout of Second Impact brought catastrophe to the world and Humanity. In the imidi... Oh God No!

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Mon Dec 18, 2006 6:49 pm I don't really know if I'd be an Eva (OR anime) fan, then. I might have forgotten it by then, lol. Seriously though, I do consider myself an otaku despite not feeling the need to see or read every single anime/manga out there, so because I only like a select few, maybe. *shrugs* It'll be just like with Nebukawa no sensei refrenced above. @ Legend of the Past: I love your Kaworu-kun avatar.

[]:             Quote Post by Auriel » Mon Dec 18, 2006 7:10 pm That would be interesting, old anime fans often are more fun to be around than young anime fans, As they've seen the evolution of anime and whatnot.

[]:             Quote Post by Kyle » Mon Dec 18, 2006 11:20 pm BobBQ wrote: Besides the oil crises will happen sometime in the next 10 years or so and anime will be the last thing on your mind.

[]:             Quote Post by Hunter21 » Tue Dec 19, 2006 5:10 pm If a day like that ever comes (which it won't) I will be glad to have people like you around. It would make the obituaries fun to read. i.e. Fan, Anime Another loser anime fan who thought he could be a badass with a gun died today. Tragically he shot himself accidently while carrying around a gun with the safety off trying to be cool. Sadly the victim did not realize that just because he saw someone use a gun in an anime did not in fact make him an expert with firearms. Authorities have contacted the victim's family and passed their reports onto the good people at the Darwin Awards as they are sure the victim is a shoe in for a award.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Tue Dec 19, 2006 5:58 pm Darwin Awards is right. :) /silliness

[]:             Quote Post by Kyle » Tue Dec 19, 2006 9:37 pm Hunter21 wrote: If a day like that ever comes (which it won't) I will be glad to have people like you around. It would make the obituaries fun to read. i.e. Fan, Anime Another loser anime fan who thought he could be a badass with a gun died today. Tragically he shot himself accidently while carrying around a gun with the safety off trying to be cool. Sadly the victim did not realize that just because he saw someone use a gun in an anime did not in fact make him an expert with firearms. Authorities have contacted the victim's family and passed their reports onto the good people at the Darwin Awards as they are sure the victim is a shoe in for a award.

[]:             Quote Post by Kaysow » Wed Dec 20, 2006 10:16 am Kyle wrote: For all you know i could be the son of a well trained assasin.
